# How Are Lightness and Brightness Different?

**Source:** [Color Nerd](https://www.youtube.com/@ColorNerd1) (YouTube Shorts)
**Date:** 2023-04-03
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/shorts/5qbtjJe1V2o
**Views:** 16,032

## Description

Physical demonstration using a painted cube to show the difference between lightness (relative to illumination) and brightness (absolute, independent of context).

## Transcript

I built this cube to demonstrate the difference between lightness and brightness. This patch of paint here — you can see as I put a shadow over it, it adjusts in its appearance. We perceive lightness as a proportion of what light is being reflected off. But we perceive brightness independently of the surrounding lighting.

So this guy, I'm going to change him because I'm playing a trick on here... and now because of this situation where this side of the cube is in shadow, this patch of pink paint now appears to be glowing with its own brightness. But in fact... it's not.

## Key Concepts

- **Lightness** — perceived as a proportion of reflected light relative to the surrounding illumination; contextual, adapts to lighting conditions
- **Brightness** — perceived independently of surrounding lighting; absolute sense of luminous intensity
- **Demonstration:** same paint patch appears to "glow" when surrounding context (shadow on cube face) tricks the visual system into interpreting it as self-luminous (bright) rather than just light-colored (high lightness)
- **Relevance to color spaces:**
  - L\* in CIELAB/OKLCH = lightness (relative)
  - V in HSV = value (loosely brightness)
  - Y in XYZ = luminance (physical brightness)
